meta.description: Complete reference of all supported bot events. Each entry shows the event name, trigger condition, available $get[] variables, and an example code snippet. Intended for documentation generation and developer reference.
usage_notes: Events with a `code` field use the CenzoJS scripting runtime ($function[args] syntax).
Events with an `execute` field use raw JavaScript (async function).
To activate a code-based event, set `code: your script` in the corresponding events/*.js file.
Variables listed under `variables` are accessible via $get[varName] inside the code string.
The standard Discord context ($userID, $channelID, $guildID, etc.) is also available in most code events.

events.name: ready
events.trigger: Fires once when the bot successfully connects to Discord.

example.description: Log startup info and set a custom presence.

events.name: guildCreate
events.trigger: Fires when the bot joins a new server.

example.description: Send a welcome message to the server's system channel.

events.name: onMessage
events.trigger: Fires every time a non-bot user sends a message in any channel.

variables.name: content
variables.description: The raw message content (also accessible via $message).

events.notes: Standard message context is fully available — $authorID, $channelID, $guildID, $username, etc.

events.name: onMemberJoin
events.trigger: Fires when a new member joins the server.

variables.name: memberID
variables.description: The new member's Discord user ID.

variables.name: memberTag
variables.description: The member's tag (User#0000 or display name).

variables.name: memberAvatar
variables.description: URL of the member's avatar image.

variables.name: memberCount
variables.description: Total server member count after this join.

example.description: Send a welcome embed to the system channel.
example.code:
$title[Welcome!]
$color[#57F287]
$description[Welcome to **$get[guildName]**, $userMention! You are our **#$get[memberCount]** member.]
$thumbnail[$get[memberAvatar]]
$footer[We're glad to have you!]
$timestamp

events.name: onMemberLeave
events.trigger: Fires when a member leaves or is kicked from the server.

variables.name: memberID
variables.description: The leaving member's Discord user ID.

variables.name: memberCount
variables.description: Total server member count after they left.

example.code:
$title[Goodbye]
$color[#ED4245]
$description[$bold[$get[memberTag]] has left **$get[guildName]**. We now have $bold[$get[memberCount]] members.]
$thumbnail[$get[memberAvatar]]
$timestamp

events.name: onMemberUpdate
events.trigger: Fires when a guild member's profile changes (nickname, roles, timeout, etc.).

variables.name: oldNickname
variables.description: Nickname before the change (empty string if none).

variables.name: newNickname
variables.description: Nickname after the change (empty string if none).

events.name: onBanAdd
events.trigger: Fires when a member is banned from the server.

variables.name: banReason
variables.description: The audit log ban reason, or 'No reason provided'.

events.notes: The context channel is the server's system channel.

example.description: Log a ban to a moderation log channel.

events.name: onBanRemove
events.trigger: Fires when a user is unbanned from the server.

events.name: onMessageDelete
events.trigger: Fires when a message is deleted.

variables.name: deletedContent
variables.description: The deleted message content. Empty if the message was not cached.

variables.name: deletedAuthorID
variables.description: The Discord ID of the message author.

variables.name: deletedChannelID
variables.description: The channel the message was deleted from.

variables.name: deletedMessageID
variables.description: The Discord ID of the deleted message.

events.notes: Discord only provides content for cached messages. Very old or uncached messages will have empty deletedContent.

events.name: onMessageEdit
events.trigger: Fires when a message is edited.

variables.name: oldContent
variables.description: Message content before the edit. Empty if not cached.

variables.name: editedAuthorID
variables.description: The Discord ID of the message author.

variables.name: editedChannelID
variables.description: The channel the message is in.

events.name: onReactionAdd
events.trigger: Fires when a user adds a reaction to any message.

variables.name: reactionEmoji
variables.description: The emoji name (Unicode or custom emoji name).

variables.name: reactionEmojiID
variables.description: The custom emoji ID. Empty for Unicode emoji.

variables.name: reactorID
variables.description: User ID of who added the reaction.

variables.name: reactedMessageID
variables.description: The ID of the message that was reacted to.

variables.name: reactedChannelID
variables.description: The channel the message is in.

variables.name: reactionCount
variables.description: Total count of this reaction on the message.

events.notes: Requires MESSAGE_CONTENT and MESSAGE_REACTIONS intents. Useful for reaction-role bots.

example.description: Log starred messages (starboard trigger when 3+ stars).

events.name: onReactionRemove
events.trigger: Fires when a user removes a reaction from a message.

variables.name: reactionEmojiID
variables.description: Custom emoji ID. Empty for Unicode.

variables.name: reactorID
variables.description: User ID of who removed the reaction.

variables.name: reactionCount
variables.description: Remaining count of this reaction on the message.

example.description: Detect when a reaction is removed.
example.code: <@$get[reactorID]> removed their $get[reactionEmoji] reaction from a message by <@$get[reactedAuthorID]>.

events.name: onChannelCreate
events.trigger: Fires when a new channel is created in the server.

events.name: onChannelDelete
events.trigger: Fires when a channel is deleted from the server.

events.name: onChannelUpdate
events.trigger: Fires when a channel is modified (name, topic, permissions, etc.).

variables.name: oldChannelName
variables.description: The channel name before the update.

variables.name: newChannelName
variables.description: The channel name after the update.

events.name: onRoleCreate
events.trigger: Fires when a new role is created in the server.

variables.name: roleColor
variables.description: The role's hex color code (e.g. '#FF0000' or '#000000').

variables.name: rolePosition
variables.description: The role's position in the role list.

variables.name: roleMentionable
variables.description: 'true' if the role is mentionable, 'false' otherwise.

events.name: onRoleDelete
events.trigger: Fires when a role is deleted from the server.

events.name: onRoleUpdate
events.trigger: Fires when a role is edited (name, color, permissions, etc.).

variables.name: oldRoleName
variables.description: The role name before the update.

variables.name: newRoleName
variables.description: The role name after the update.

variables.name: oldRoleColor
variables.description: The hex color before the update.

variables.name: newRoleColor
variables.description: The hex color after the update.

events.name: onGuildUpdate
events.trigger: Fires when the server's settings change (name, icon, region, etc.).

variables.name: oldGuildName
variables.description: The server name before the change.

variables.name: newGuildName
variables.description: The server name after the change.

variables.name: memberCount
variables.description: Total member count at the time of the event.

events.name: onVoiceStateUpdate
events.trigger: Fires when a member joins, leaves, or moves between voice channels.

variables.name: oldChannelID
variables.description: The channel they were in. Empty if they just joined.

variables.name: newChannelID
variables.description: The channel they moved to. Empty if they just left.

variables.name: oldChannelName
variables.description: Name of the channel they were in.

variables.name: newChannelName
variables.description: Name of the channel they moved to.
